div.p: The Rule (SHA-256 schedule recurrence): W[t] = σ1(W[t-2]) + W[t-7] + σ0(W[t-15]) + W[t-16] C: W[t] = expr; ← wrap is free Python/JS: W[t] = expr & 0xFFFFFFFF ← mask is mandatory

p.em: (33 bits — does not fit in uint32_t)

div.p: Result: 0x00000001 ✓ uint32_t c = a + b; No masking needed — C standard guarantees wrap at 2³²
True mathematical result 4294967297 (0x100000001) Python integers are arbitrary precision
Result: 4294967297 ✗ c = a + b # BUG — no mask 33-bit value propagates into next schedule word computation
Cascade failure: W[t] = σ1(...) + W[t-7] + σ0(...) + W[t-16] Each subsequent W word accumulates extra bits → entire schedule diverges from FIPS 180-4 from W[16] onward
True mathematical result 4294967297 (0x100000001) Python holds the full value...
Apply mask immediately after addition c = (a + b) & 0xFFFFFFFF ▲ This line is the ENTIRE fix & 0xFFFFFFFF zeroes all bits ≥ 32
Result: 0x00000001 ✓ Identical to C uint32_t result. Treat & 0xFFFFFFFF as the second half of the addition operator.
